title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
summary: AI engineers are moving away from manual prompting toward automated loops where agents prompt agents in orchestrated workflows. Cole Medin describes building a TypeScript loop system using Pi that runs continuously with database observability, featuring an orchestrator spawning parallel worker agents.
writeup: The video covers a shift in agent development away from hand written prompts toward automated loops where agents prompt other agents in orchestrated workflows. Cole Medin describes building a TypeScript loop system using Pi that runs continuously with database observability, structured around an orchestrator that spawns parallel worker agents. The framing connects to test-time compute and multi-agent systems: rather than crafting individual prompts, engineers design the loop and let orchestration distribute work. The takeaway is a concrete architecture (continuous loop, observability through a database, an orchestrator coordinating parallel workers) that reframes prompt engineering as system design.
